summary: DHH announces that Basecamp has reached tens of thousands of users across over 40 countries. He emphasizes that this was achieved with only about two and a half full-time team members and without adding any staff to scale. The post promotes an upcoming 'Building of Basecamp' workshop in San Francisco. It serves as both a milestone celebration and an advertisement for their consulting events.
key_points: Basecamp reached tens of thousands of users in over 40 countries with a team of roughly 2.5 full-time people
Scaling without adding personnel was an intentional design goal from day one — build a product that doesn't require organizational scaling
The product serves a wide range of users from individuals managing home projects to businesses managing client work
37signals was already monetizing their methodology through paid workshops like 'Building of Basecamp'
money_quotes: We're extremely proud to have gotten this far with a team of what amounts to about two and a half full timers.
What's even more amazing is that we've been able to scale without adding a single person.
That was one of the goals from day one: Build a product that doesn't require organizational scaling.
key_insight: A deliberately small team can build and scale a product used by tens of thousands if you design from day one to avoid organizational scaling.
